structure.text: 1. The external project solves a narrower and harder problem than generic loop engineering.
2. TeaPrompt already covers the reasoning layer but under-specifies the compiler layer.
3. Pure text skills are not enough for high-risk repeatable workflows.
4. A full runner would be premature.
5. Workflows / Router Flow — roles under a budget
Implementation Lessons (if ever adopting the concept)
Follow-up (2026-06-17): verified against the upstream repo
Required Risk Block for Future Mentions
Plan: Code Follow-ups for Prompt Library
Manual (optional, LLM-cost): compare baseline vs skill-assisted runs
1. Regenerate committed prompt/skill index when docs, skills, or plans change
TASK-003: Optional upstream follow-up if hands-on TRINITY is desired
Training Recipes (from docs/source, not independently retrained)
Benchmark Tiers (from `results/README.md` — read these by tier)
What Runs Today vs What Is Blocked
Research Synthesis: Prompting to Skills as Workflow
I: ROUTE-002 holdout as merge gate?
J: Localized cues beyond TW cheatsheet?
K: Expand ROUTE-002 with fresh holdout cases?
M: Run 23-task benchmark eval in CI?
P: More ROUTE-002 holdout before router tuning?
U: Sync stale QUALITY_GATES / holdout metrics?
V: zh-TW ROUTE-002 holdout without full SKILL i18n?
W: Re-open SKILL.zh-TW.md companions or tenth skill?
Rounds 8–20 — Milestone close and maintenance layer (2026-06-25)
Round 8 — X: Close Governance layer maturity milestone?
Round 9 — Y: Close Project judgment layer milestone?
Round 11 — AA: CI gate for skill examples?
Round 13 — AC: Root AGENTS harness pointer?
AK: Open implementation blockers from Rounds 8–20?

AV: TW adopters need panel link?
AW: Update QUALITY_GATES metrics and GLOSSARY playbook?
BB: Close extended panel at Round 35?
BE: Plan-only and production-negation rules in `route_paraphrase_eval.py`?
BG: Codify production-negation and plan-only as normative?
BI: TW fairness groups for plan-only and plain review?
BJ: Operational definitions for plan-only and plain review?
CE: Force minimality on every implement?
CG: ROUTING_CONTRACT R12 — boundary quick-cue summary?
CH: Probe-linked anti-drift for quick-cue block?
CI: Expand quick cues (minimality, dispatch-meta, design comparison)?
CK: QUALITY_GATES 7.4 mentions cheatsheet parity tests?
CL: Re-litigate backlog (tenth skill, default minimality, benchmark CI, full i18n)?
CP: Expand ROUTE holdout or tune router?
CS: Sync library README panel record?
CU: Root README North Star + governance pointers?
CX: Expand ROUTE holdout / tune router?
CY: GLOSSARY playbook `make all` + round bump?
DB: Expand to all prompt categories now?
DS: Expand to `06-repo/` Purpose sweep now?
EA: Complete all nine workflow skill ↔ thinking-lens cross-links?
EB: Skill Module Contract anti-drift for all nine skills?
ED: Router / holdout / tenth skill / benchmark CI?

skills.name: reflective-dispatch
skills.description: Use this when a user asks to apply the Reflective Prompt Library, convert prompts into skills or workflows, choose prompt-only vs artifact vs agentic workflow, or route a non-trivial task before execution. It selects the smallest useful reflective workflow and keeps Doing the right thing > doing things right.

frontmatter.name: reflective-dispatch
frontmatter.description: Use this when a user asks to apply the Reflective Prompt Library, convert prompts into skills or workflows, choose prompt-only vs artifact vs agentic workflow, or route a non-trivial task before execution. It selects the smallest useful reflective workflow and keeps Doing the right thing > doing things right.

skills.name: reflective-minimality
skills.description: Use this as a Minimality Gate or anti-bloat review before implementation, during refactoring, or when an agent may overbuild. It challenges whether code should exist, prefers deletion, standard library, platform-native features, existing dependencies, one-line or one-file solutions, and requires intentional shortcuts to carry explicit ceiling and upgrade triggers.

frontmatter.name: reflective-minimality
frontmatter.description: Use this as a Minimality Gate or anti-bloat review before implementation, during refactoring, or when an agent may overbuild. It challenges whether code should exist, prefers deletion, standard library, platform-native features, existing dependencies, one-line or one-file solutions, and requires intentional shortcuts to carry explicit ceiling and upgrade triggers.

skills.name: reflective-brief
skills.description: Use this to start non-trivial tasks, clarify real goals, define assumptions, scope, acceptance criteria, falsifiability, and the minimal next step before planning or implementation. Especially useful when a request is broad but safe to proceed with explicit assumptions.

frontmatter.name: reflective-brief
frontmatter.description: Use this to start non-trivial tasks, clarify real goals, define assumptions, scope, acceptance criteria, falsifiability, and the minimal next step before planning or implementation. Especially useful when a request is broad but safe to proceed with explicit assumptions.

skills.name: reflective-research
skills.description: Use this for current external research, official documentation checks, DeepWiki inspection, long-document synthesis, platform comparison, or source-backed recommendations. It separates evidence from inference and avoids dumping raw context.

frontmatter.name: reflective-research
frontmatter.description: Use this for current external research, official documentation checks, DeepWiki inspection, long-document synthesis, platform comparison, or source-backed recommendations. It separates evidence from inference and avoids dumping raw context.

structure.text: State Ledger (Final, Optional for short tasks)

skills.name: reflective-review
skills.description: Use this to review code, diffs, specs, plans, AI outputs, articles, or decisions. It combines critical thinking, counterargument, test integrity, spec traceability, and actionable required fixes.

frontmatter.name: reflective-review
frontmatter.description: Use this to review code, diffs, specs, plans, AI outputs, articles, or decisions. It combines critical thinking, counterargument, test integrity, spec traceability, and actionable required fixes.

skills.name: reflective-implement
skills.description: Use this for coding, refactoring, debugging with edits, or repo-aware implementation tasks. It enforces small safe changes, acceptance criteria traceability, tests, verification, and a final report without weakening requirements.

frontmatter.name: reflective-implement
frontmatter.description: Use this for coding, refactoring, debugging with edits, or repo-aware implementation tasks. It enforces small safe changes, acceptance criteria traceability, tests, verification, and a final report without weakening requirements.

skills.name: reflective-handoff-retro
skills.description: Use this when a task needs context handoff, session transfer, retrospective, reusable memory, process learning, or conversion of repeated experience into rules, checklists, skills, scripts, or tests.

frontmatter.name: reflective-handoff-retro
frontmatter.description: Use this when a task needs context handoff, session transfer, retrospective, reusable memory, process learning, or conversion of repeated experience into rules, checklists, skills, scripts, or tests.

skills.name: reflective-risk
skills.description: Use this before high-risk work involving security, privacy, auth, permissions, data deletion, migrations, production, billing, legal/medical/financial stakes, or irreversible decisions. It creates a dry-run, rollback, and Human Review gate before execution.

frontmatter.name: reflective-risk
frontmatter.description: Use this before high-risk work involving security, privacy, auth, permissions, data deletion, migrations, production, billing, legal/medical/financial stakes, or irreversible decisions. It creates a dry-run, rollback, and Human Review gate before execution.

skills.name: reflective-spec-plan
skills.description: Use this to turn a requirement into a practical spec, usage-first design, reviewable task plan, no-code Test Plan, or agent workflow design. Trigger when the user asks for a spec, PRD, tickets, implementation plan, workflow plan, stateful or resumable workflow specification, rigorous test design before implementation, or wants to avoid building the wrong thing.
